response.justification: The candidate is a versatile senior full-stack engineer with broad experience across web, mobile, and systems programming, but falls well short of the core minimum qualifications for this role. C++ experience is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments (~7 months) and a thesis project, far from the required 8 years. Large-scale infrastructure exposure exists (high-throughput DLang microservices at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic, Kubernetes deployments), but most of the career centers on product-level web development rather than cloud compute platforms, and there is no formal technical-leadership track record mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers.
strengths: Hands-on low-latency, high-throughput systems work (millions of HTTP requests/sec) at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering-practice culture: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus teaching via workshops and courses
Broad polyglot experience with cloud-adjacent tooling (AWS CDK, Docker, Kubernetes) and long-running open-source ownership
concerns: C++ experience is roughly under a year, versus the 8-year minimum requirement
No demonstrated technical-leadership role leading strategy or mentoring cloud/DevOps teams
Career focus is product/web development, not cloud compute platforms or distributed-systems architecture at platform scale
